dialogos.texto_en: Oh, it's Ichika.\nWhat are you looking at?
Hey...\nWhat're you doing in front of the Awamori shop?
Ichika, drinking alcohol is NOT good, you know.
Huh?
Oh, Futaro-kun and Nino?\nThat's surprising.
I know that Futaro-kun.\nDon't worry, it's not for me.
Is it a souvenir for our father?
Yeah, I thought Awamori would be good\nas it's a typical Okinawan souvenir.
Yesterday, Yotsuba was thinking the same thing.\nBut I don't think I've ever seen Dad drinking alcohol.
Ah, now that you mention it...\nI start wondering if he can drink alcohol at all.
(Come to think of it,\n he said he didn't like alcohol before.)
(Well, he might have said that\n just to refuse Dad's invitation...)
Well, you guys can't drink alcohol.\nWhy not choose something that everyone can eat?
Why everyone?
Well, while eating it with your family you could talk about the \nmemories you made during this trip.
As a family...
Oh, that's a good idea.\nWe can turn the memories into souvenirs too.
That's a really thoughtful suggestion,\nnot something I'd expect from you, Futaro-kun.
Well, it's just my opinion.\nPlease consider it, if its useful to you.
Futaro, you care about our father, don't you?
Well, yeah, of course.
(I even took Nino to the hospital\n during the school festival.)
(And above all, at that time,\n I was told to seriously think about\n my relationship with his daughters...)
Someday, we have to report him that we're dating.
Futaro...
Oh yeah, you two are so innocent.
Even though it's not official yet,\ndoes Dad know that you're dating?
I wonder.
You can't just talk about it\nlike it's someone else's problem, come on.
Well, I understand that you're afraid of our dad.
I-I'm not afraid of him, or anything.
Really? You don't feel anything at all?
...I have an aversion to him, that's for sure.
So you admit it?
But, I'm not afraid of him!
Don't force yourself.\nWe too get nervous sometimes\nwhen we're talking to him.
Ah, right!\nHow about greeting him with an Okinawan souvenir?\nWhat do you think, Futaro-kun?
Well, as a starting point, it might be a good idea.
You don't need to say anything, Nino.\nI'll explain things myself.
Oh? Give it your best shot Futaro-kun.
Of course, I'll give my best!\nBesides, even if Nino tells her dad,\nwe won't be able to gain his trust.
Futaro...!
Teehee, he'll make a decision when the time is right.\nNino is a lucky girl.
Hey, don't make fun of me.
I'm not making fun of you,\nI really think that way.
Ah, now I can send off my cute little sister\nwith peace of mind...!
You're really not making fun of me, right?
Yes, I really mean it.\nYou'll make Nino happy, right Futaro-kun?
Y-yeah. Of course, leave it to me.
W-wait, Miku!\nWhy did the conversation suddenly turned this way?
Ah, right!\nHow about greeting him with an Okinawan souvenir?\nWhat do you think, Futaro-kun?
Well, as a starting point, it might be a good idea.
He probably senses it.
Why do you think that?\nDad left us alone and only returned six months ago.
He'll sense it because he's your father.
You're starting to understand it too, aren't you?
(Even if you're not blood-related,\n your dad has deep feelings for you guys...)
...That's right.\nHe is our dad after all.
But we still need some time to get used to it.
It's...a two-way street.\nFor Dad and for us.
It's only been half a year,\nso we still have a lot of catching up to do.
Ah, right!\nHow about greeting him with an Okinawan souvenir?\nWhat do you think, Futaro-kun?
Well, as a starting point, it might be a good idea.
However, with my current financial situation,\nI might not be able to afford anything decent.
Buying a local souvenir just from anywhere\nmight be considered rude...
We just graduated from high school,\nso there's nothing to worry about.
Besides, it's not about the money.\nIt's the thought that counts, the THOUGHT.
The point is to show that the souvenir\nis one that you, Futaro-kun,\nhas chosen to give Dad.
Ugh...\nThe pressure is on...
(Well, at any rate,\n I'll have to properly greet their father someday.)
